item.title: Women replace men in fuel queue in Chuadanga
item.description: WOMEN in Chuadanga have begun lining up at fuel stations alongside men to collect diesel, as families attempt to keep agricultural activities running amid supply pressures....

item.title: BATTERY-RUN RICKSHAWS: Sangram Parishad demands immediate registration
item.description: The council for movement for battery-run rickshaws and ‘easy bikes’ on Sunday demanded the inclusion of registering their vehicles along with issuing licences for the drivers of these vehicles in the government’s 180-day priority action plan....

item.title: HADI MURDER: Further probe report submission deferred for 11th time
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday extended the deadline for submitting the report of further investigation in the case of the killing of Inquilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi by 16 more days....Geographic Reference

item.title: I was abducted, tortured over Facebook post, businessman tells ICT
item.description: A businessman on Sunday told the International Crimes Tribunal-1 that he was abducted, secretly detained, and tortured in 2020 over Facebook posts critical of the then government and India....

item.title: Ctg Anwara plant stops DAP fertiliser production over ammonia shortage
item.description: Production at the country’s lone state-owned di-ammonium phosphate fertiliser factory at Anwara upazila in Chattogram has been suspended following a shortage of ammonia, the key raw material for the plant....

item.title: ‘1971 Shei Shob Din’ to be screened in Texas
item.description: The acclaimed Bangladeshi film ‘1971 Shei Shob Din,’ directed by Hridi Huq, is scheduled to be screened at the Cinemark Pflugerville 20 and XD in Texas, USA, today....

item.title: ‘Janantike’ depicts memories, roots
item.description: Visual artist Fuleshwary Priyanandini has depicted memories, emotions, and roots in her fourth solo exhibition, currently under way at Gallery Illusion in Dhanmondi, Dhaka....

item.title: CUET architecture dept holds project exhibition
item.description: The architecture department of Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology held a project exhibition at the CUET in Chattogram on Sunday....

item.title: Multidisciplinary artist Abu Jafar’s solo held in London
item.description: London-based Bangladeshi multidisciplinary artist Abu Jafar’s solo exhibition, titled On My Way, concluded at the HSoA Gallery in London on Friday....

item.title: UIU holds HSIL Hackathon 2026
item.description: The United International University organised the 7th edition of the Harvard Health Systems Innovation Lab Hackathon 2026 at the Institute of Research, Innovation, Incubation and Commercialisation of UIU in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: IBBL clients demand asset recovery, S Alam arrest
item.description: Islami Bank customers at a human chain in front of the bank’s head office on Sunday demanded arrest of loan defaulter S Alam, recovery of his laundered money and cancellation of Section 18 (ka) of the Bank Resolution Law......

item.title: MCCI for supportive FY27 budget
item.description: Businesses called for a ‘supportive and growth-oriented’ national budget for the 2026–27 fiscal year, saying that entrepreneurs remain under severe strain due to high inflation, sluggish investment, elevated interest rates, and foreign exchange pressure......

item.title: Govt will not accept any IMF term adverse to people, businesses: Khasru
item.description: Regarding status of loans and cooperation, finance and planning minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Sunday said that the government would not accept any conditions that create undue pressure on the public or the business community......

item.title: Prosecution for indictment of Shamim Osman, 11 others
item.description: The prosecution on Sunday prayed to the International Crimes Tribunal-1 to frame charges against former Awami League lawmaker Shamim Osman and 11 others for their alleged involvement in crimes against humanity committed during the July 2024 uprising....

item.title: Govt urged to appoint FBCCI administrator from business community
item.description: Top business leaders from various sectors urged the government on Sunday to appoint a business community administrator to the Federation of Bangladesh Chambers of Commerce and Industry ahead of the election......

item.title: Zelensky slams oil sanctions relief for Russia
item.description: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky on Sunday condemned the easing of sanctions on Russian oil after the United States extended a waiver meant to soften surging energy prices driven by the Middle East war......

item.title: Nationwide measles vaccination begins today
item.description: Bangladesh is set to begin a nationwide measles-rubella vaccination campaign today amid an ongoing outbreak of the disease....

item.title: Chartered secretaries urged to uphold integrity, economy
item.description: Describing chartered secretaries as the nation’s economic defence team, Chowdhury Ashik Mahmud Bin Harun, executive chairman of the Bangladesh Investment Development Authority, on Sunday underscored the profession’s vital role in upholding corporate integrity and national economic stability......

item.title: Humans far behind as robot breaks record at Beijing half marathon
item.description: A humanoid robot competing against flesh-and-blood runners broke the world record at a Beijing half marathon on Sunday, showcasing the rapid technological advancement achieved by Chinese makers......

item.title: China seeks to rein in risks from AI ‘digital humans’
item.description: After her father died from cancer, Zhang Xinyu had an artificial intelligence avatar made that looks and sounds just like him, part of a growing ‘digital human’ industry that China is moving to govern more tightly......

item.title: How France fell for reimagined 19th-century workers’ canteens
item.description: So-called bouillon restaurants are mushrooming all over France, reviving a traditional low-cost Gallic meal concept that can compete with fast-food on prices and easily beat it on quality......

item.title: Muktadir seeks Canadian investment in solar power
item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Sunday invited Canadian entrepreneurs to invest in the country’s solar power sector as a primary vehicle for future bilateral trade growth......

item.title: AI ‘agent’ fever comes with lurking security threats
item.description: Artificial intelligence ‘agents’ promise to save users time and energy by automating tasks, but the growing power of systems like OpenClaw is setting cybersecurity experts on edge......

item.title: 5 pvt banks brought on board BIDA’s One-Stop Service platform
item.description: The Bangladesh Investment Development Authority on Sunday signed memoranda of understanding with five private commercial banks to integrate them into its One Stop Service or OSS platform, expanding the digital investment facilitation ecosystem......

item.title: 3 more hired at secy rank on contract
item.description: The government has appointed three more former bureaucrats to the posts of director general of the NGO Affairs Bureau, chairman of the Land Appeal Board, and Waqf Administrator at the Bangladesh Waqf Administration Office with the status of secretary....

item.title: Afzal elected REHAB president
item.description: Ali Afzal has been elected president of the Real Estate and Housing Association of Bangladesh for the 2026-2028 term, said a press release......

item.title: Dhaka Bank signs loan deal with Toyota Bangladesh
item.description: Dhaka Bank PLC has recently signed a memorandum of understanding with Toyota Bangladesh Limited to establish a strategic partnership aimed at strengthening auto financing services for customers, said a press release......Geographic Reference

item.title: Indian refiners pay for Iranian oil in yuan
item.description: Indian refiners are making payments using Chinese yuan for the limited cargoes of Iranian oil they are buying under the temporary US sanctions waver, according to a report by Reuters, says NDTV on Saturday....

item.title: Mastercard announces winners of Safari Spirit campaign
item.description: Mastercard has recently announced winners of its ‘Safari Spirit’ campaign 2025-26, said a press release......

item.title: StanChart, Channel i launch 11th Agrow Award
item.description: Standard Chartered Bangladesh, in partnership with Channel i, on Sunday launched the 11th edition of the Agrow Award, continuing their joint effort to recognise individuals and organisations contributing to the development of Bangladesh’s agriculture sector......

item.title: NRB Bank signs deal with United Healthcare
item.description: NRB Bank PLC has recently entered into a strategic partnership with United Healthcare Services Limited, further strengthening its commitment to delivering enhanced healthcare benefits to its valued customers and employees, said a press release......

item.title: Islami Bank relocates Trishal branch
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC has shifted its Trishal branch to Abdur Rashid Chairman Market, holding no 4170, Main Road, ward no 2, Trishal Municipality, Trishal in Mymensingh, said a press release......

item.title: Trust Bank among top 10 remittance-collecting banks
item.description: Trust Bank PLC has been recently won ‘Top Ten Remittance Award 2026’ at Branding Bangladesh Beyond Bangladesh event organised by Centre for NRB at Pan Pacific Sonargaon Hotel in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: 64 initiatives win Retail Awards 2026
item.description: A total of 64 accolades were presented in 26 categories at the 3rd edition of Shwapno presents Bangladesh Retail Awards powered by Singer and Bata, in association with Konka and Gree (Electro Mart Limited), at Radisson Blu Water Garden Hotel in the capital on Saturday......

item.title: Price hike brings no respite from fuel consumers’ sufferings
item.description: Consumers on Sunday underwent the same old suffering — long queues at different Dhaka city filling stations — despite paying increased prices for fuel oils....

item.title: MDB signs deal with Axentec for Tier-4 Cloud tech
item.description: Midland Bank PLC has recently signed a memorandum of understanding with Axentec PLC, a subsidiary of Robi Axiata, said a press release......

item.title: Housing for people, not cars
item.description: EVERY two years, people from around the world convene at the World Urban Forum to discuss urbanization issues. This May, the World Urban Forum will be held in Baku, Azerbaijan, where representatives of government and civil society will focus on how to provide housing to growing urban populations......

item.title: Education as a promise of peace
item.description: EDUCATION is frequently presented as one of the most powerful instruments for building a peaceful and prosperous world. Few ideas carry as much weight in global policy discourse as the belief that education can prevent conflict and cultivate tolerance across societies. At a surface level, this proposition appears both intuitive and compelling. Education is widely......

item.title: Quiet shift towards coercion at India’s borders
item.description: IN 2025, border killings along the Bangladesh–India frontier reached a five-year high, with at least 34 Bangladeshi nationals killed by India’s Border Security Force, according to the Bangladeshi human rights organisation Ain o Salish Kendra. For years, such incidents were described as unfortunate excesses at a difficult border, episodic and manageable within the......

item.title: Current measles outbreak
item.description: Measles is caused by a virus, which belongs to a family of virus known as paramyxoviridae. These viruses are enveloped in two layers of lipid, which make it fragile to heat, acidity or causticity and sunlight but because of non-segmented genome, it is relatively more stable than its cousin influenza virus, orthomyxoviridae. This also incidentally helps to develop a stable......

item.title: Institutional neglect undermines solar success
item.description: THE use of solar power for irrigation projects in a single season in Rangpur has saved around 7.5 million litres of diesel. This shows that solar projects deliver where they are functional, especially at a time of fuel uncertainty. Amid global energy market volatility triggered by US-Israel war on Iran, the agriculture sector is under strain, especially during the irrigation season......

item.title: Renewed campus violence worrying
item.description: THE government of the Bangladesh National Party appears non-committal about containing violence in educational institutions. Mymensingh Medical College on April 18 postponed all classes and examinations for a week and asked students to vacate hostels after a clash between two factions of the Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of the ruling party. The clash......

item.title: Fuel ‘looted’ from Rangpur station after attack
item.description: A complaint has been filed with the local police station alleging that 167 litres of petrol have been looted from a filling station in Pirganj upazila of Rangpur after launching an attack on it....

item.title: Coast Guard arrests ‘smuggler’ with Indian ‘Red Bull’
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested a smuggler, who smuggled a large quantity of Indian energy drinks, Red Bull, evading customs duties, at Shibu Market under Fatulla in Narayanganj early Sunday...

item.title: SC gets two chamber judge courts
item.description: The chief justice of Supreme Court of Bangladesh on January 6, 2026 nominated justice Md Rezaul Haque as judge-in-chamber-1 and justice Farah Mahbub as judge-in-chamber-2 to dispose of emergency matters of the Appellate Division until further orders....

item.title: Two jailed for life, another for 14yrs
item.description: A Women and Children Repression Prevention Tribunal in Dhaka on Sunday awarded life sentence to two men and a 14-year jail sentence to another man for the kidnap and murder of an 11-year-old boy 18 years ago....Geographic Reference

item.title: FACTIONAL CLASH AT MMC: JCD expels 2 leaders, forms probe body
item.description: Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, has expelled two of its Mymensingh Medical College unit leaders over Saturday’s factional clash at the college that compelled the institution’s authorities to suspend classes......

item.title: Worker dies from electrocution in Jhenaidah
item.description: A WORKER died after being electrocuted while carrying out painting work at an under-construction building in Jhenaidah town on Sunday....

item.title: Zelensky slams oil sanctions relief for Russia
item.description: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky on Sunday condemned the easing of sanctions on Russian oil after the United States extended a waiver meant to soften surging energy prices driven by the Middle East war....

item.title: Jamaat protests at fuel price hike
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Sunday protested at and expressed deep concern over the fuel price hike, saying that it would increase the sufferings of the common people....

item.title: Blue Origin launches rocket with used booster for first time
item.description: Blue Origin, the US space company of Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, on Sunday successfully reused and recovered a booster for its New Glenn rocket, confirming its mastery of a technical feat that could boost its launch cadence and expand its rivalry with SpaceX....

item.title: College principal attacked at his office
item.description: The principal of Government Adiabad Islamia High School and College, Nur Sakhawat Hossain, was allegedly attacked by a group of miscreants at his office at Raipura upazila in Narsingdi on Sunday....

item.title: Three US university students hurt in shooting
item.description: Three students at the University of Iowa were wounded in a shooting off campus early Sunday, the school president said....

item.title: College student killed, 25 hurt in Habiganj villagers’ clash
item.description: A college student was killed and more than 25 people were injured in a clash between two groups of villagers over the possession of a piece of khas land in Nabiganj upazila of Habiganj on Sunday....

item.title: JnU Badhan hosts workshop and freshers’ reception
item.description: The Jagannath University unit of Badhan hosted an organisational workshop alongside Freshers’ Reception 2026, aiming to strengthen members’ skills and welcome new volunteers into the fold....

item.title: National interest first in any NCT handover to foreign firm: minister
item.description: Shipping minister Shaikh Rabiul Alam on Sunday said that the government would prioritise national interest in taking any decision regarding the issue of handing over the Chattogram Port’s New Mooring Container Terminal to a foreign company....

item.title: Labour crisis hits Sunamganj boro harvesting
item.description: The local administration of Tahirpur upazila in Sunamganj ordered the closure of three land customs stations for 12 days, from April 20 to May 1, to tackle the labour crisis during the ongoing boro paddy harvest....

item.title: State minister seeks UK investment to develop fisheries, livestock
item.description: State minister for fisheries and livestock Sultan Salahuddin Tuku on Sunday sought investment from the United Kingdom to develop the country’s fisheries and livestock sectors....

item.title: DOMESTIC WORKERS’ REGISTRATION: HC questions govt inaction
item.description: The High Court on Sunday issued a rule asking why the authorities’ inaction in implementing a mandatory registration and regulatory system for domestic workers under the Domestic Servants Registration Ordinance, 1961 should not be declared illegal....
